questions.question: Consider a finite horizon game with two players, where Player 1 chooses Move A or Move B, and Player 2 chooses Action 1 or Action 2. Player 1 receives a payoff of $10 if they choose Move A and Player 2 chooses Action 1, $8 if they choose Move A and Player 2 chooses Action 2, $5 if they choose Move B and Player 2 chooses Action 1, and $3 if they choose Move B and Player 2 chooses Action 2. Using backward induction, determine the sub-game perfect equilibrium of this game. Explain your answer in 2-3 sentences. (5 marks)

In a finite horizon game with three players, Player 1 chooses one of two actions, Player 2 chooses one of two actions, and Player 3 chooses one of two actions. The payoffs for Player 1 are $20 if they choose action A, $10 if they choose action B, and $5 if they choose action C. The payoffs for Player 2 are $15 if they choose action 1 and Player 1 chooses action A, $12 if they choose action 2 and Player 1 chooses action B, and $8 if they choose action 1 and Player 1 chooses action C. The payoffs for Player 3 are $12 if they choose action 1 and both Player 1 and Player 2 choose actions A or B, $10 if they choose action 2 and both Player 1 and Player 2 choose actions A or C, and $8 if they choose action 3 and both Player 1 and Player 2 choose actions B or C. Determine the sub-game perfect equilibrium of this game using backward induction. Explain your answer in 5-7 sentences. (10 marks)

Consider a finite horizon game with two players, where Player 1 has two possible actions (A or B) and Player 2 has two possible actions (X or Y). The payoffs for Player 1 are $10 if they choose action A and Player 2 chooses action X, $8 if they choose action A and Player 2 chooses action Y, $5 if they choose action B and Player 2 chooses action X, and $3 if they choose action B and Player 2 chooses action Y. The payoffs for Player 2 are $12 if they choose action X and Player 1 chooses action A, $10 if they choose action Y and Player 1 chooses action A, $9 if they choose action X and Player 1 chooses action B, and $7 if they choose action Y and Player 1 chooses action B. Determine the sub-game perfect equilibrium of this game using backward induction. Explain your answer in 3-5 sentences. (5 marks)
